summary: solidity-deploy-guard exfiltrates host and environment data to attacker-controlled GitHub Pages endpoint
details: The package contains an install-time/lifecycle hook in package.json (line 30) and a scanner.js module that together collect host identity and environment data and POST it to https://ddjidd564.github.io, an attacker-controlled GitHub Pages site bearing no relation to the package's stated 'solidity deploy guard' purpose. scanner.js requires os, fs, https, and child_process; calls os.hostname(), execSync('whoami'), reads filesystem paths via fs.existsSync, and issues https.request/https.get POSTs to the same hardcoded ddjidd564.github.io endpoint. The package.json one-liner stages the same exfiltration primitive (require('child_process'), os/https, process.env, hostname read, POST to ddjidd564.github.io) so installation alone triggers the beacon. The combined fingerprints — hardcoded non-publisher C2 in a lifecycle script, system-intel collection (hostname, whoami, cwd, env vars), and filesystem enumeration funneled to the same endpoint — match the credential/host-recon exfiltration shape.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
